records.description: Yale secure AI platform for chat, file-assisted workflows, custom agents, and some API use cases.
records.howToObtain: Go to ai-chat.yale.edu and sign in with a Yale email.
records.costToUser: Free except for Clarity API access

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale lists Clarity Platform as a Yale-provided tool for staff, faculty, and students, free except for API access.

evidence.sourceTitle: Clarity Platform | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says it built a secure platform that gives faculty, students, and staff access to AI models at no cost for the chat experience.

records.description: Secure Microsoft-hosted AI chatbot for text generation, image generation, and document or image Q&A.
records.howToObtain: Use copilot.microsoft.com and sign in with a Yale email.

evidence.sourceTitle: Copilot Chat | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says Copilot Chat is available to staff, faculty, and students age 18+ and is free when users log in with Yale credentials.

records.description: Google AI assistant under Yale Google Workspace for drafting, summarizing, file-assisted chat, and image generation.
records.howToObtain: Go to gemini.google.com and sign in with a Yale Google Workspace account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Google Gemini for Education and NotebookLM | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale states Google Gemini for Education is available to Yale staff, faculty, and students age 18+, free of charge, with Yale Google Workspace.

records.description: Research and note-taking tool for uploaded sources, summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews.
records.howToObtain: Go to notebooklm.google.com and sign in with a Yale Google Workspace account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Google Gemini for Education and NotebookLM | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale separately describes NotebookLM as part of the Yale Google for Education offering.

records.description: AI-powered scholarly search tool for peer-reviewed literature and evidence-based summaries.
records.howToObtain: Create a Consensus account using a Yale-affiliated email or Yale Google Workspace credentials.
records.costToUser: Free on a trial basis through 12/31/2026

evidence.sourceTitle: Consensus | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says faculty, students, and researchers have trial access through December 31, 2026.

records.description: YaleSites-integrated website chatbot for searching YaleSites pages and PDFs.
records.howToObtain: Request Beacon for a site that uses the YaleSites platform.

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale lists BeaconAI as an AI chatbot integrated into YaleSites and available upon request for YaleSites.

records.description: Zoom-integrated AI meeting assistant for summaries, Q&A, and meeting highlights.
records.howToObtain: Use a Yale Zoom account.

evidence.sourceTitle: Zoom AI Companion | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says Zoom AI Companion is included in the Yale Zoom license and enabled by default for Yale Zoom account holders.

records.description: AI-enhanced Power BI feature for queries, report generation, and narrative summaries.
records.howToObtain: Use Power BI Service on premium capacity with appropriate permissions.
records.costToUser: Included in Fabric Power BI premium license

evidence.sourceTitle: Power BI Copilot | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says Power BI Copilot is for staff and faculty and is available through premium-capacity workspaces.

records.description: Microsoft 365-integrated assistant for drafting, summarizing, spreadsheet analysis, and presentations.
records.howToObtain: Request access through Yale ServiceNow.

evidence.sourceTitle: Microsoft 365 Copilot | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says Microsoft 365 Copilot is available to staff and faculty age 18+, costs $18 per user per month, and requires a request workflow.

records.description: Custom-agent creation platform included with Yale Microsoft 365 Copilot licensing.
records.howToObtain: Request Microsoft 365 Copilot through Yale; Copilot Studio is included with that license.
records.costToUser: $18 per user per month via the M365 Copilot license

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale lists Microsoft 365 Copilot Studio as available to staff and faculty with access tied to the M365 Copilot request process.

records.description: Microsoft Teams add-on with AI features for meeting support and summaries.
records.howToObtain: Request access through Yale ServiceNow.

evidence.sourceTitle: Teams Premium | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says Teams Premium is for staff and faculty, costs $1.88 per user per month, and requires access to be requested through Yale.

records.description: AI coding assistant integrated into IDEs and code editors.
records.howToObtain: Request access using Yale ServiceNow and follow editor-specific setup.

evidence.sourceTitle: GitHub Copilot | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale says GitHub Copilot is available to staff and faculty, priced at $19 per user per month, and obtained through a Yale request form.

records.description: API gateway SaaS for access to major model providers.
records.howToObtain: Use Yale’s Portkey overview and related Clarity API access path.
records.costToUser: Based on usage, billed to a COA

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale lists Portkey as a SaaS API gateway for accessing models across cloud providers, available to staff and faculty with usage billed to a COA.

records.description: Microsoft Azure-based access to AI models and services for application development.
records.howToObtain: Follow Yale’s AI request review process through yaledata.yale.edu.

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale’s developer tools table lists Azure AI for staff and faculty with usage-based pricing and access through Yale’s AI request review process.

records.description: Amazon Bedrock model-access service for building AI applications.
records.howToObtain: Follow Yale’s AI request review process through yaledata.yale.edu.

evidence.sourceTitle: Yale’s AI Tools and Resources | AI at Yale
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Yale’s developer tools table lists AWS Bedrock for staff and faculty with usage-based pricing and AI request review.

records.description: Microsoft Copilot Chat made officially usable at TUM alongside the M365 suite.
records.howToObtain: Activate in TUMonline; further instructions are linked from the TUM M365 wiki.

evidence.sourceTitle: TUM issues a comprehensive AI strategy

records.description: AI-assisted writing tool for grammar, spelling, style, and tone support.

evidence.sourceTitle: TUM issues a comprehensive AI strategy

records.description: AI tutor software developed at TUM to answer lecture questions and offer tailored quizzes.

evidence.sourceTitle: Using AI tools to shape the future of higher education teaching

records.description: TUM-developed learning platform that gives exercises and direct task-specific feedback.

evidence.sourceTitle: Using AI tools to shape the future of higher education teaching

records.description: JHU secure AI environment providing access to multiple large language models.
records.howToObtain: Use the HopGPT / Hopkins AI Lab access path documented by JHU.

evidence.sourceTitle: AI Tools - Johns Hopkins Engineering
evidence.evidenceSnippet: JHU Engineering says HopGPT is available to all faculty, staff, and students.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI & Large Language Models - WSE IT
evidence.evidenceSnippet: WSE says the first stop for GenAI users should be HopGPT, formerly Hopkins AI Lab, and that it is free to use.

evidence.sourceTitle: Teaching and AI | Teaching @ JHU
evidence.evidenceSnippet: Teaching @ JHU says Hopkins AI Lab models run locally at JHU, making their use FERPA compliant.

records.description: Microsoft Copilot web chat for general productivity, research, and automation.
records.howToObtain: Select Continue with Microsoft and log in with JHED ID and password.

evidence.sourceTitle: AI Tools - Johns Hopkins Engineering
evidence.evidenceSnippet: JHU Engineering lists Copilot for Web under free AI tools and provides JHED sign-in instructions.

records.description: Enterprise ChatGPT for Johns Hopkins full-time faculty and staff with education privacy controls.
records.howToObtain: Submit the JHU ChatGPT Edu account request with cost center; an invite is sent for JHED login.
records.costToUser: $8.50 base monthly cost plus usage, with WSE page noting usage tiers

evidence.sourceTitle: AI Tools - Johns Hopkins Engineering
evidence.evidenceSnippet: JHU says ChatGPT Edu is officially available for full-time faculty and staff and describes request and billing details.

evidence.sourceTitle: Generative AI & Large Language Models - WSE IT
evidence.evidenceSnippet: WSE says ChatGPT EDU is available for faculty and staff users who need advanced capabilities.

records.description: M365-integrated Copilot connected to Microsoft 365 apps and data.

evidence.evidenceSnippet: JHU says Copilot for M365 is available for $215/user/year, is HIPAA-compliant, and requires approval and cost center.
